description: agent-symbiont fleet: self-evolving binaries reporting evolution pipeline health, LLM token consumption and revision registry state. Designed for many concurrent instances -- filter via the model/instance variables; all timeseries break down per instance. Summary quantiles are unpopulated (report 0), so means are derived from _sum/_count rates. Events arrive in bursts (a refinement round every ~2-3 min), so all rates/ratios/means use fixed 30m windows -- short windows render as unreadable combs and zero-valued stats. The Training harness / Pareto frontier / Persistence rows cover the agent_symbiont_* series emitted by the agent-symbiont harness on top of the framework.
title: symbiont

panels.description: Number of symbiont instances currently reporting metrics (matching the model/instance filters).
panels.title: Instances

panels.description: Completed agentic LLM runs per hour across the selected fleet, smoothed over a 30-minute window.
panels.title: LLM runs / h

panels.description: Fraction of evolution attempts that produced an activated revision (activations / attempts). The complement is the failure ratio (parse / compile / signature / no_rust_code).
panels.title: Attempt success ratio

panels.description: Failed evolution attempts per hour across the fleet (all failure kinds).
panels.title: Evolve failures / h

panels.description: Mean wall-clock duration of Runtime::evolve calls (including retries), derived from the summary _sum/_count.
panels.title: Mean evolve duration

panels.description: Cumulative LLM output tokens reported by the selected fleet.
panels.title: Cumulative output tokens

panels.description: Failed evolution attempts per hour by failure kind (parse / compile / signature / no_rust_code), summed across selected instances. Compile failures dominating suggests prompt or crate-context issues.
panels.title: Evolve failures / h by kind

panels.description: Revision activations per hour per instance -- the fleet's effective evolution throughput.
panels.title: Revision activations / h by instance

panels.description: Mean attempts per Runtime::evolve call per instance (1 = first-try success). Rising values mean the model needs more retries to produce loadable code.
panels.title: Mean attempts per evolve by instance

panels.description: Mean duration per evolution pipeline stage (llm / parse_validate / compile / load), derived from summary _sum/_count. Log scale: llm dominates by orders of magnitude.
panels.title: Mean stage duration

panels.description: Mean wall-clock Runtime::evolve duration (including retries) per instance. Slow instances point at contended LLM backends or slow compile hosts.
panels.title: Mean evolve duration by instance

panels.description: Token consumption per second by kind (input / cached_input / output), summed across selected instances, smoothed over 30m. cached_input is cheap; watch fresh input + output for cost.
panels.title: Token rate by kind

panels.description: Total token consumption per second per instance (all kinds). Identifies which fleet members burn the most LLM capacity.
panels.title: Token rate by instance

panels.description: Mean tokens and messages per agentic run (fleet-wide, from summary _sum/_count). Growing input tokens per run = context bloat in the evolution prompts.
panels.title: Mean tokens & messages per run

panels.description: Completed agentic runs per hour by outcome, plus transient LLM retries per hour. Anything other than `ok` deserves a look at the logs; rising retries point at a flaky LLM backend.
panels.title: LLM runs / h by outcome

panels.description: Revisions retained in the keep-all registry per instance. Grows monotonically; watch alongside dylib size for disk usage (~49 MB per dylib observed).
panels.title: Revisions loaded & active id by instance

panels.description: Mean generated Rust source size and compiled dylib size per revision (fleet-wide, from summary _sum/_count). Log scale: dylibs are ~4 orders of magnitude larger than their source.
panels.title: Mean artifact size per revision

panels.description: Backtest throughput across selected instances, summed over splits (train/test) and phases (fresh rounds / frontier re-evaluation).
panels.title: Backtests / s

panels.description: Fraction of backtests aborted early because the evolved code panicked. A rising ratio means the LLM generates crashier code.
panels.title: Backtest panic ratio

panels.description: Completed refinement rounds per hour (evolve + evaluate + persist), all outcomes.
panels.title: Rounds / h

panels.description: Fraction of refinement rounds that completed (outcome ok); the complement aborted the training run on a failed evolution.
panels.title: Round success ratio

panels.description: Candidates retained on the pareto frontier, summed across selected instances. The gauge drains to 0 every round, so this shows the 30m maximum instead of the instantaneous (mostly 0) sample.
panels.title: Frontier candidates

panels.description: Time since the most recent successfully completed training run. Training runs daily; alert past ~26h.
panels.title: Since last successful train

panels.description: Backtest throughput broken down by dataset split (train/test) and phase (fresh candidate rounds vs. frontier re-evaluation). rate() of a counter, robust across process restarts.
panels.title: Backtests / s by split & phase

panels.description: Backtests aborted early by a panic in the evolved code, per hour. Should be near zero; spikes correlate with prompt or prelude changes.
panels.title: Backtest panics / h by split & phase

panels.description: Mean wall-clock duration of one evaluation pass (all datasets x all matrix configurations), derived from _sum/_count rates.

targets.expr: sum by (split, phase) (rate(agent_symbiont_evaluation_duration_seconds_sum{model=~"$model",instance=~"$instance"}[30m])) / sum by (split, phase) (rate(agent_symbiont_evaluation_duration_seconds_count{model=~"$model",instance=~"$instance"}[30m]))

panels.title: Mean evaluation duration by split & phase

panels.description: Mean wall-clock duration of one full refinement round (evolve + train/test evaluation + persistence). The evolve share alone is symbiont_evolve_duration_seconds.
panels.title: Mean round duration by instance

panels.description: Candidates retained on the pareto frontier per instance. The raw gauge drains to 0 every round (comb pattern), so the 30m max envelope is plotted alongside it; envelope pinned at pareto_capacity means the eviction path is active.
panels.title: Frontier size by instance

panels.description: Pareto frontier verdicts for freshly evolved candidates (phase=round). The entered share is the key signal that the agentic loop still produces improving candidates.
panels.title: Verdicts / h by verdict (fresh candidates)

panels.description: Refinement rounds per hour by outcome (ok / evolve_failed / error), 30m smoothed.
panels.title: Rounds / h by outcome

panels.description: Frontier verdicts per hour across all phases. phase=round covers fresh candidates; phase=frontier_reeval appears when retained candidates are re-scored on the slid dataset window (never emitted so far, hence the all-phase breakdown instead of a permanently empty phase filter).
panels.title: Frontier verdicts / h by phase & verdict

panels.description: Median surefire ratio of every candidate retained on the pareto frontier, by rank (0 = best, descending). The raw gauge drains to 0 every round and vacated ranks are reset to 0, so the 30m max envelope is plotted. The spread between rank 0 and the worst rank shows frontier diversity; ranks collapsing onto each other means the frontier holds near-duplicates.
panels.title: Frontier surefire ratios by rank (30m max)

panels.description: Objectives of the best retained candidate per instance: reward = median excess-return surefire ratio, risk = p95 daily equity drawdown. The raw gauges drain to 0 every round, so the 30m max envelope is plotted. A non-positive reward means only a reference seed is retained.
panels.title: Best candidate objectives by instance

panels.description: Quality-gate criteria violated by evaluated candidates, per hour by criterion (30m smoothed, phase=round). Only liquidation and invalid_metrics (SAFETY) reject a candidate; the rest are advisory feedback. The dominating criterion is the gate to tune or the defect the agent must engineer away.
panels.title: Quality gate failures / h by criterion

panels.description: Mean ClickHouse insert latency by store (backtest results, generated candidates, evolve failures), derived from _sum/_count rates.
panels.title: Mean insert duration by store

panels.description: Failed ClickHouse inserts per hour by store. Any non-zero rate stalls or aborts training runs.
panels.title: Insert failures / h by store
